known_issues: App fails to load after deployment; likely caused by routing/auth initialization or JavaScript error on startup

existing_features.title: Add a top-level error boundary in App.tsx that catches JavaScript errors during initial render and displays a fallback UI instead of a blank screen.
Guard the App router initialization so that the authentication state from InternetIdentityProvider is fully resolved before any route component renders, showing a loading spinner in the meantime.

Make the LandingPage 'Get Started' button resilient to auth initialization errors: wrap the login call in a try/catch, display a user-friendly error toast on failure, and never leave the button in a permanently disabled or silent-fail state.

Wrap the useActor hook's actor creation in a try/catch so that a failure to instantiate the backend actor does not crash the app; instead, queries that depend on the actor should gracefully return an empty/error state.

projectName: Fix app loading failure with robust error handling and auth initialization
